titleStmt.title: SII 28.11: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 11. (A.R. No. 11 of 1913.) PERAMBALŪR, PERAMBALUR TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: Records that for celebrating a festival with the tittam (ceremonial and final function) on the day of Bharaṇi (in the month of Āvaṇi every year ?), the tirunāḷ (the birthday asterism) of Teṉṉavaṉai-muḻudum-uḍaiyāḷ, the queen of the king (nam-pirāṭṭiyār), the ūravar of Perambalūr assigned as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭa-iraiili) (tax free land in the enjoyment of a Vishṇu temple) in perpetuity 10 vēli, out of the land which had always remained uncultivated or waste (aṉaittāṇḍum-pāḻ-kiḍanda) to the south of Perambalūr and another plot of 2 vēli, for raising a flower garden (tiru-nanda-vana-ppuṟa-iṟai-ili) (tax free land which had attached to the temple garden). On the order of the ūravar, Iḷavēḷūr-uḍaiyāṉ Vaḻittuṇai-pperumāḷ, the accountant of the ūr wrote this and also attested it. [other members of the line also attested the record but this portion of the inscription is damaged].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . perumāḷ cuntarapāṇ
ṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉeḻāvatiṉ
Etirāmāṇṭu Āvaṇi māta mutalāka nam
pirāṭṭiyār teṉṉavaṉai muḻutumuṭaiyā
ḷ tirunāḷ paraṇiyile tittamāka Iṉṉāyaṉār
tirunāḷ Eḻuntaruḷa Amutupaṭi cāttupaṭi U
ḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum Avaiyiṟṟukku perum
paḷūr teṉ parappil ciṟupāccūr vaḻikku me
ṟkum kiḻakkum Aṉaittāṇṭum pāḻkiṭa
ṉta nilattil patiṟṟu veliyum Ināya
ṉāṟku tirunantavaṉappuṟa Iṟaiyilikku nila
m Iruveliyum Ākanilam paṉṉiruveli
yum tiruviṭaiyāṭṭa Iṟaiyili Āka tiruk
kai malarile Utakam paṇṇikkuṭuttom
Ippaṭikku Ivvolaiye piṭipāṭākak
koṇṭu cantirātittavaṟ kāṇiyākat ti
ruvāḻi Āḻvārai Eḻuntaruḷavittu kalve
ṭṭik kuṭuttom perumpalūr Ūravarom
Ippaṭikku Ivai Ivvūravar paṇiyāl Ūrk
kaṇakku Iḷaveḷūruṭaiyāṉ vaḻittuṇai
p perumāḷ Eḻuttu . Ippaṭikku Ivai perumThe latter half of line 21 and further lines upto 32 are very much damaged (erased ?)
Digital edition of SII 28.11 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
